name: Nowack & Olson, PLLC
description_short: Miami-based bankruptcy law firm with multiple Florida locations, offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy representation and free consultations.
description_long:
Nowack & Olson, PLLC is a bankruptcy law firm headquartered in Miami, Florida, with additional office locations in Plantation, Boca Raton, and Jupiter. The firm is led by two named attorneys: Mitchell Jordan Nowack, Esq. and Christian James Olson, Esq. The firm specializes exclusively in bankruptcy law, providing legal representation to individuals and businesses navigating Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy proceedings.

The firm offers free initial consultations to prospective clients, making it accessible for individuals exploring bankruptcy options without upfront costs. They accept credit card payments for their services, providing flexible payment arrangements. All four office locations can be reached through a single phone number, (954) 349-2265, and the firm maintains a dedicated website at floridabankruptcynow.com for additional information.

The primary distinction of Nowack & Olson is their geographic footprint across South Florida's most populous areas, with strategic office locations spanning from Miami to Jupiter—a service radius covering approximately 75 miles. This multi-location approach allows them to serve clients across different communities without requiring travel to a single downtown office. The firm's dedicated bankruptcy practice (rather than a general practice with bankruptcy as one offering) suggests focused expertise in this specific legal domain.

The firm maintains no visible client reviews on FindLaw as of the listing update date (03/03/2026), which neither confirms nor denies quality of service. The website content is minimal, providing basic contact and location information without detailed case studies, attorney biographies, or client testimonials. Prospective clients should independently verify attorney credentials and conduct disciplinary research before engagement, as recommended by FindLaw's own guidance.
pros: Multiple office locations across South Florida (Miami, Plantation, Boca Raton, Jupiter) for client convenience
Free initial consultations available to all prospective clients
Accepts credit card payments for flexible fee arrangements
Dedicated bankruptcy specialization rather than general practice
Single contact number across all four office locations simplifies client communication
Named principals (Mitchell Jordan Nowack and Christian James Olson) with listed attorney profiles
cons: No client reviews available on FindLaw to assess service quality or outcomes
Minimal online presence with no detailed attorney biographies or case information
No information about fee structure, payment plans, or estimated costs provided on listing
Limited website content makes it difficult to assess firm size, experience, or specific bankruptcy expertise
No mention of specific bankruptcy chapter specialization or case outcome data
best_for: South Florida residents seeking free bankruptcy consultations without geographic barriers
Individuals filing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy in Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach counties
Clients preferring to work with a bankruptcy-focused firm rather than general practice attorneys
diagnosis: Best for South Florida residents seeking bankruptcy legal representation with convenient office access and free initial consultations. Primary caveat: minimal online documentation and no visible client reviews make independent credential verification and disciplinary research essential before hiring.
services: Multi-location legal representation across South Florida
Credit card payment options for legal services
Attorney consultations with named principals Mitchell Jordan Nowack and Christian James Olson
